heading: Research, development, demonstration, and commercial application activities
label: 13541 Research, development, demonstration, and commercial application activities

children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this Act, research, development, and demonstration activities under this Act may be carried out under the procedures of the Federal Nonnuclear Research and Development Act of 1974 (0), the Atomic Energy Act of 1954 ( et seq.), or any other Act under which the Secretary is authorized to carry out such activities, but only to the extent the Secretary is authorized to carry out such activities under each such Act. An objective of any demonstration program under this Act shall be to determine the technical and commercial feasibility of energy technologies.42 U.S.C. 5901–59242 U.S.C. 2011

Except as otherwise provided in this Act, in carrying out research, development, and demonstration programs and activities under this Act, the Secretary may use, to the extent authorized under applicable provisions of law, contracts, cooperative agreements, cooperative research and development agreements under the Stevenson-Wydler Technology Innovation Act of 1980 [ et seq.], grants, joint ventures, and any other form of agreement available to the Secretary.15 U.S.C. 3701

children.heading: Commercial application
children.label: b Commercial application
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this Act, in carrying out commercial application programs and commercial application activities under this Act, the Secretary may use, to the extent authorized under applicable provisions of law, contracts, cooperative agreements, cooperative research and development agreements under the Stevenson-Wydler Technology Innovation Act of 1980 [ et seq.], grants, joint ventures, and any other form of agreement available to the Secretary. An objective of any commercial application program under this Act shall be to accelerate the transition of technologies from the research and development stage.15 U.S.C. 3701

children.heading: “Joint venture” defined
children.label: c “Joint venture” defined
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “joint venture” has the meaning given the term “joint research and development venture” under section 4301(a)(6) and (b) of title 15, except that such term may apply under this section to research, development, demonstration, and commercial application joint ventures.

children.heading: Protection of information
children.label: d Protection of information
children.text: Section 12(c)(7) of the Stevenson-Wydler Technology Innovation Act of 1980 [], relating to the protection of information, shall apply to research, development, demonstration, and commercial application programs and activities under this Act.15 U.S.C. 3710a(c)(7)

entering into a contract, cooperative agreement, cooperative research and development agreement under the Stevenson-Wydler Technology Innovation Act of 1980 [ et seq.], grant, joint venture, or any other form of agreement available to the Secretary under this section that relates to research, development, demonstration, and commercial application; or15 U.S.C. 3701

extending a contract, cooperative agreement, cooperative research and development agreement under the Stevenson-Wydler Technology Innovation Act of 1980, grant, joint venture, or any other form of agreement available to the Secretary that relates to research, development, and demonstration to cover commercial application.

children.heading: Application of section
children.label: f Application of section
children.text: This section shall not apply to any contract, cooperative agreement, cooperative research and development agreement under the Stevenson-Wydler Technology Innovation Act of 1980 [ et seq.], grant, joint venture, or any other form of agreement available to the Secretary that is in effect as of .15 U.S.C. 3701October 24, 1992
